summary: Package fetches payload from anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket
details: dist/index.js line 14 contains a fetch() to https://pub-0dc3e1677e894f07bbea11b17a29e032.r2.dev — an anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket — combined with reads of process.platform and process.versions for platform-targeted payload selection. Anonymous *.r2.dev buckets are mutable, attacker-controllable hosting with no version pinning, integrity verification, or publisher attribution; this same infrastructure pattern (pub-<hex32>.r2.dev) has been used in prior npm cluster compromises to drop platform-native binaries. Legitimate runtime/SDK fetches occur from publisher-owned or well-known CDNs (storage.googleapis.com for Chromium, the publisher's own GitHub releases at a pinned version), not from anonymous R2 buckets keyed on the host platform. Installer impact: arbitrary attacker-controlled bytes are pulled onto the installer's machine and the host fingerprint (platform, Node version) is leaked to the attacker's bucket logs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
